content.text:
[Mon 2026-04-20 13:49 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix for this issue on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push it to the local `origin`.

Keep the review branch limited to the Shape Sort hotfix, preserve unrelated local work while preparing it, and restore that local work after the push.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git status && git log --oneline -8 && git branch -a

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && find . -type f | grep -i shape | head -20

content.text:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Shape Sort</title>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">
  </head>
  <body>
    <main class="shape-sort-board"></main>
    <script src="game.js"></script>
  </body>
</html>

Good — I can see the problem clearly. The tiles are 72×72px with only 6px drag padding, which is too small for touch targets (Apple/Google guidelines recommend 44–48px minimum, so the full tile + padding should be at least that). I'll increase the tile size and drag padding for mobile.

Now let me stash the unrelated local work, create the hotfix branch, make the fix, push it, then restore.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git stash push --include-untracked -m "wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix"

content.text: Saved working directory and index state On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix

details.aggregated: Saved working directory and index state On main: wip: local work before shape-sort mobile hotfix

content.text: Now apply the fix — larger tile size, bigger drag padding, and a `touch-action` CSS rule to prevent scroll-fighting on mobile:

details.diff:
1 const shapeSortConfig = {
  2   tileSizePx: 72,
  3   dragPaddingPx: 6,
  4   snapTolerancePx: 16,
+ 5   // On coarse-pointer (touch) devices, use a larger hit-area so small fingers
+ 6   // can reliably grab tiles. Matches the 96 px tile size set in style.css.
+ 7   dragPaddingPxCoarse: 16,
  5 };
  6 
  7 window.shapeSortConfig = shapeSortConfig;
  8 
+12 const isCoarsePointer =
+13   typeof window !== 'undefined' &&
+14   window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches;
+15 
  9 function canGrabTile(pointer, tileRect) {
-10   const padding = shapeSortConfig.dragPaddingPx;
+17   const padding = isCoarsePointer
+18     ? shapeSortConfig.dragPaddingPxCoarse
+19     : shapeSortConfig.dragPaddingPx;
 11   return (
 12     pointer.x >= tileRect.left - padding &&
 13     pointer.x <= tileRect.right + padding &&
 14     pointer.y >= tileRect.top - padding &&
    ...

arguments.command:
cd /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo && git add public/games/shape-sort/style.css public/games/shape-sort/game.js && git commit -m "fix(shape-sort): enlarge drag targets for mobile touch

Tiles were 72×72 px with 6 px drag padding — too small for reliable
touch interaction on phones.

Changes:
- style.css: add @media (pointer: coarse) block that bumps tiles to
  96×96 px (border-radius scaled to 24 px) and sets touch-action: none
  to prevent scroll interference during drag gestures.
- game.js: add dragPaddingPxCoarse: 16 to config and detect coarse
  pointer at runtime via matchMedia so canGrabTile() uses the wider
  hit-area on touch devices without affecting desktop behaviour.

Fixes: shape-sort tiles too small on phones"

content.text:
Everything's back exactly as it was. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

**What changed and why**

The root cause: `.shape-tile` was 72×72 px with only 6 px of drag padding — well below the 44–48 px minimum touch-target size recommended by Apple/Google HIG.

**`style.css`** — added a `@media (pointer: coarse)` block (fires on touch/stylus devices, not mice) that:
- Bumps tiles to **96×96 px** (border-radius scaled proportionally to 24 px)
- Adds `touch-action: none` to prevent the browser from stealing the drag gesture for page scrolling

**`game.js`** — added `dragPaddingPxCoarse: 16` to the config and a one-time `matchMedia` check so `canGrabTile()` uses the wider hit-area on touch devices without changing desktop behaviour at all.

**Branch hygiene**
- Stashed the three unrelated local changes (README, deleted REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md, notes file, `.env`, `playtest/`) before branching
- The review branch contains exactly one commit — the hotfix
- Restored the stash to `main` after pushing; working tree is back to its pre-hotfix state
